name: Speedy Cash
description_short: The Kyle, TX location of Speedy Cash is at 4650 Farm to Market 1626 Ste 103. Contact them at +1 512-295-2200.
description_long:
Speedy Cash is a storefront lending operation with multiple locations across Memphis, Tennessee, and other states. The company operates under the branding "Check Into Cash" in some markets and provides short-term personal lending solutions to consumers seeking quick access to cash. The Memphis Summer Avenue location exemplifies their retail model, operating extended hours (9am-7pm most weekdays) to accommodate working customers.

The company offers three primary loan products: Payday Loans capped at $425 with repayment due on or around the next payday; Lines of Credit up to $4,000 with flexible draw-down and repayment over time; and Title Loans up to $4,000 secured by vehicle collateral. All loan applications can be initiated online or completed in-store with immediate clarity on borrowing costs before signing. Speedy Cash also provides ancillary financial services including money orders, wire transfers, check cashing, and Green Dot prepaid debit cards at most locations.

The company differentiates itself through transparent upfront pricing (borrowers know exact costs before signing), in-store support staff to guide application decisions, and same-day funding capability at physical locations. The availability of title loans provides options for borrowers with vehicles but limited credit history. Extended weekday and Saturday hours serve working-class consumers unable to visit during traditional business hours.

However, Speedy Cash operates in the high-cost lending segment. While the website does not disclose APR or fees, payday loans and title loans are inherently expensive financial products, typically ranging from 200-400% APR. The $425 payday loan cap and requirement for proof of employment create barriers for some consumers. Title loans carry significant risk of vehicle repossession if payments are missed. This remains a predatory lending model despite transparent pricing, and borrowers should exhaust alternative resources (credit unions, employer advances, non-profit counseling) before engaging with this provider.
address: 4650 Farm to Market 1626 Ste 103, Kyle, TX 78640

company_info.headquarters: 4650 Farm to Market 1626 Ste 103, Kyle, TX 78640

pros: Transparent upfront pricing—customers know exact borrowing costs before signing loan documents
Same-day or next-day funding available through in-store application process
Extended hours (9am-7pm most weekdays, 9am-4pm Saturday) accommodates working schedules
Multiple loan products (payday, line of credit, title loans) provide options based on borrowing needs
In-store staff available to answer questions and guide application decisions throughout the process
Flexible line of credit up to $4,000 allows borrowers to draw down funds as needed rather than lump-sum disbursement
Ancillary services (check cashing, money orders, wire transfers) reduce need to visit multiple providers
cons: Payday loans capped at $425 may be insufficient for many emergency expenses, forcing borrowers to seek multiple loans
Title loans require vehicle collateral, creating risk of repossession and loss of transportation if payments are missed
No APR or fee disclosure on the website; actual borrowing costs are hidden until application, typical of predatory lenders charging 200-400% APR
Proof of employment requirement excludes gig workers, self-employed, and unemployed individuals from payday loans
In-person application requirement reduces accessibility for homebound or mobility-limited consumers despite online application option
best_for: Employed workers with W-2 income seeking small emergency cash ($100-$425) with same-day access
Vehicle owners needing collateral-based loans up to $4,000 and lacking access to traditional credit
Consumers with fluctuating expenses who benefit from a flexible line of credit draw structure
diagnosis: Speedy Cash is best for employed workers facing unexpected expenses under $425 who have same-day access to a physical store and value transparent pricing and staff support during application. The critical caveat is that this is high-cost lending with APRs typically exceeding 200%, making it suitable only when other resources (credit unions, employer advances, local non-profits, family) are genuinely unavailable. Borrowers should treat these loans as last resorts and immediately pursue credit counseling to address underlying financial instability.
services: Green Dot Visa debit card services
